reviews.feedback: I think this looks okay. I think we want to make sure we test invariants, and we should also look where we can use data-driven testing. Why does this use the `.tap` syntax?
In someways this looks better. Its a little simpler. It uses the actual factories.
It should also check for soft destroyable test helpers, and things like that.

I like that it tests the side effects with hte to_change. I also like that it doesn't test every single validation, it just tests the behavior that belongs to the request. I also like that it inlines the post, it doesn't create too many lets.
Yeah this one has too many let variables, when it could just inline.
